name: Peña de la Cruz – running circular through the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de Candelario
name_original: Peña de la Cruz – running circular por el Espacio Natural de la Sierra de Candelario
region_intro: Jogging routes around Béjar traverse a diverse landscape, from historical gardens to expansive natural areas and mountainous terrain. The region is characterized by the Sierra de Candelario Natural Area, offering numerous trails with varying difficulties and significant elevation changes. Runners can explore the serene El Bosque de Béjar, an historic garden featuring oak and chestnut trees, ponds, and sinuous paths. The broader Sierra de Béjar-La Covatilla area provides rugged paths and challenging terrain, particularly during warmer months. Best jogging routes around Béjar The most popular jogging route is Peña de la Cruz – running circular through the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de Candelario, a 4.2 miles (6.7 km) trail that takes 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route features accessible paths within the natural area, with some sections requiring firm footing. Another top favourite among local runners is Cross of the Candelario Park – La Ancianita bullring loop from Béjar, a difficult 8.1 miles (13.1 km) path. This route includes a considerable elevation gain, ideal for those seeking a strenuous workout in a mountainous environment. Local runners also love the Plaza Mayor of Béjar – Ventorro Pelayo Viewpoint loop from Béjar, a 11.7 miles (18.8 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 4 minutes. Jogging around Béjar is defined by historical gardens, expansive natural areas, and mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier runs to more challenging high-mountain sections. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 250 runners have used komoot to explore Béjar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Béjar?
A: There are over 30 running routes around Béjar documented on komoot, offering a wide range of experiences for different fitness levels. These include trails through historical gardens, expansive natural areas, and mountainous terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails near Béjar?
A: The running trails around Béjar offer incredibly diverse terrain. You can find everything from accessible paths within the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Candelario Natural Area&lt;/strong&gt; to serene, sinuous paths through the historic &lt;a href="https://www.europeanhistoricgardens.eu/en/el-bosque-de-bejar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bosque de Béjar&lt;/a&gt;, which features oak and chestnut trees. For more challenging runs, the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Béjar-La Covatilla&lt;/strong&gt; area provides rugged paths and significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Béjar?
A: Yes, Béjar offers routes suitable for all levels. While many trails feature considerable elevation, you can find easier runs up to 5 km with minimal elevation gain, often on mostly paved surfaces, perfect for those new to running or looking for a lighter workout.

Q: Where can I find challenging trail running routes in the Béjar area?
A: For advanced runners seeking a strenuous workout, the mountainous environment of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Candelario Natural Area&lt;/strong&gt; and the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Béjar-La Covatilla&lt;/strong&gt; are ideal. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133368" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cross of the Candelario Park – La Ancianita bullring loop from Béjar&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.1-mile path with significant elevation gain, provide a demanding experience. For an ultra-running challenge, consider the 42.2 km circular route to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canchal de la Ceja – Torreón (Calvitero Summit) loop from Béjar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Béjar?
A: Yes, many of the running routes around Béjar are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993685637/pena-de-la-cruz-running-circular-through-the-espacio-natural-de-la-sierra-de-candelario" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de la Cruz – running circular through the Espacio Natural de la Sierra de Candelario&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4.2 miles long. Other longer circular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Béjar – Ventorro Pelayo Viewpoint loop from Béjar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running near Béjar?
A: Running routes around Béjar offer stunning natural features and landmarks. You can encounter beautiful mountain views in the Sierra de Candelario and Sierra de Béjar-La Covatilla. Highlights include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.europeanhistoricgardens.eu/en/el-bosque-de-bejar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bosque de Béjar&lt;/a&gt; with its ponds and lush forests, and viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2712929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ambroz Valley and Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. You might even discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5701178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Chorrera Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or reach summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2671693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreón (Calvitero Summit)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any running trails that pass by lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, some running routes in the Béjar area offer views of or pass by bodies of water. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31867934" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Ancianita bullring – Views on the embalse de Navamuño loop from Béjar&lt;/a&gt; provides scenic views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navamuño Dam&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5432000" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navamuño Reservoir Views&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features natural lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2653623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Duque&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2653641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trampal Lagoons&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Béjar?
A: The warmer months are generally ideal for running in Béjar, especially if you plan to explore the higher altitudes of the Sierra de Béjar-La Covatilla, where snow can be present in winter. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, making them excellent seasons for enjoying the diverse trails.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly running trails in Béjar?
A: While specific regulations for each trail should always be checked, many natural areas in Spain are generally welcoming to dogs on a leash. The varied landscapes around Béjar, including the Sierra de Candelario Natural Area and El Bosque de Béjar, often provide suitable environments for running with your canine companion. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect local signage.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Béjar?
A: The komoot community highly values the diverse natural environment of Béjar, which offers a wide spectrum of routes for runners of all abilities. Users frequently praise the breathtaking views from the mountainous trails, the serene beauty of historic gardens like El Bosque, and the variety of terrain that keeps every run interesting and rewarding.

Q: Are there options for parking near the running trailheads in Béjar?
A: Yes, many popular running routes and natural areas around Béjar offer accessible parking options. While specific parking availability can vary by trailhead, areas like the Sierra de Candelario and starting points within Béjar itself typically have designated parking to accommodate visitors.

Q: Can I reach running trails in Béjar using public transport?
A: Public transport options to reach trailheads can vary. While Béjar itself is accessible, reaching some of the more remote mountain trails might require a car. However, routes starting directly from Béjar's town center, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Béjar – Ventorro Pelayo Viewpoint loop from Béjar&lt;/a&gt;, are easily accessible if you are staying within the town or arrive via public transport to Béjar.
tips:
You can ascend from Béjar along the road, making stops at the Sagrado Corazón and Fuente del Lobo, to reach El Castañar.
At the end of the path we find a great esplanade with picnic areas and fountains that is presided over by a huge granite cross and its viewpoint located on a rock.

waypoints.name: Peña de la Cruz and Balanced Boulder
